experiences.intentions: The active L piece is near the top center, and the only settled blocks are a flat 2x2 O at the bottom centered around columns 4-5. Early game goal is to place pieces cleanly to keep the stack flat and preserve flexibility for upcoming S,T,Z,J. From the spawn orientation, a hard drop would place the L flat on top of the O, creating unnecessary height in the middle. Shifting left can set up a cleaner edge placement on later turns and avoids stacking directly over the center. Immediate threats are minimal since the board is almost empty. Among candidates, left improves future placement flexibility more than no_op or soft_drop, while rotating now is premature without first moving toward a side.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Key state: active L is near the top, bottom has a centered 2x2 O stack creating a flat platform at columns 4-5. Goal is to place the L efficiently without creating holes and ideally set up the upcoming S/T/Z/J. Immediate best sub-goal is to move the L toward the existing center structure so it can be dropped cleanly onto/next to the O block. Candidate actions: hard_drop now would freeze the current horizontal L high above center-left, which is acceptable but less flexible; soft_drop commits downward before alignment; rotations are not yet necessary because the current flat orientation can fit well; right shifts the piece toward the O stack and preserves future rotation/drop options better than left or no_op. So move right now.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: The board is nearly empty with a settled O block centered at the bottom. The active L is high and currently horizontal; there is no immediate threat, so the goal is efficient placement to keep the stack flat and set up for upcoming S/T/Z/J pieces. Dropping the current horizontal L immediately would land it on top of the O and create a wider bump, while rotating to vertical can better tuck alongside or build a cleaner column next turn. Among actions, rotate_right improves future landing shape more than moving or dropping now.
experiences.tasks: Clear as many lines as possible in Tetris by placing tetrominoes strategically.

experiences.intentions: Board is mostly empty with a flat 2x2 O block on the floor centered at columns 4-5. Active L is high and currently vertical with foot to the right. Goal is to place pieces to keep a clean, flat stack and avoid creating awkward overhangs. Since there is ample space, the strongest immediate move is to hard drop into a useful low position; in its current orientation and column, the L will fall to rest neatly on/adjacent to the O block without wasting turns, and delaying with lateral/rotation setup is less efficient this move.

Candidate actions: hard_drop would commit immediately in current orientation, likely landing awkwardly on the central unevenness without checking best fit. Left/right could align later, but first rotating to a more useful down-pointing T often improves tuck potential over the center. Rotation 2 creates a flat top with the stem down, a strong shape for settling into the central notch. So the best immediate step is to rotate clockwise toward that orientation.

experiences.intentions: Active piece is a T in vertical orientation near the top-center, with a fairly uneven stack below and no immediate danger at the top. The key opportunity is to improve placement before committing; the center-left around columns 3-5 has structures where a horizontal T could fit better than the current vertical stance. My goal is to set up a cleaner surface and preserve future placements for upcoming J,S,L,Z pieces. Among actions, moving left/right shifts position but does not address orientation; dropping now in the current vertical form is premature and likely creates a bump. Rotating right changes the T to a horizontal top with center stem below, which is a more flexible shape for fitting onto the developing center structure next turn. So rotate_right is the best immediate setup action.

Immediate goal is to place this L efficiently without creating extra overhangs while setting up upcoming Z and I. Rotating right to orientation 1 makes the L vertical with a foot to the right, which aligns better for sliding/dropping into the central gap than the current flat-top spawn; moving first is less committal but misses a strong setup, and dropping now in current orientation would leave a broader overhang near the top.
